mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in FEDFUNDS signals easier policy and lower discount rates, which supports equity multiples and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS declines are followed by negative 20-day SPX returns more often than positive returns over any 3-year rolling window, or if Rank IC stays below 0.03 in that regime.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%, further tightening raises the discount rate and compresses risk appetite, so S&P 500 returns are negative over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:policy rate was near the effective lower bound in 2015
failure_condition: Reject if, after FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, the next-5-day SPX return is positive in more than 55% of cases or the average return is not negative across the sample.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates weakening household income and deteriorating earnings expectations, which translates into lower S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE upshocks above 0.3 percentage points in 3 months are not followed by negative 20-day SPX returns at least 60% of the time, or if the signal's rolling Rank IC is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in RSAFS and HOUST captures broad domestic demand acceleration; when consumption and housing improve together, S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint RSAFS and HOUST strengthening does not outperform either series alone and fails to produce positive 60-day SPX returns in at least 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in INDPRO indicates stronger industrial output and better cyclical revenue momentum, which supports S&P 500 gains over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive month-over-month INDPRO changes are followed by non-positive 20-day SPX returns more than half the time over any 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while GDPC1 growth is slowing, real discount rates and margin pressure rise together, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6m annualized > prior 6m and GDPC1 growth slowing
failure_condition: Reject if inflation acceleration paired with GDP deceleration is not associated with negative 60-day SPX returns in at least 60% of occurrences, or if the interaction term is insignificant in rolling tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in PAYEMS signals labor-market income growth and stronger spending capacity, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days through improved growth expectations.
failure_condition: Reject if PAYEMS increases above 0.2% month-over-month are not followed by positive 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the average 5-day return is not above zero.

mechanism_narrative: A low FEDFUNDS rate combined with falling CPIAUCSL inflation signals a benign disinflationary easing environment, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if low-rate disinflation regimes do not produce positive 20-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the combined signal fails to beat either component alone.
